entries.title: Homily on Acts 50 on acts 23:35
entries.excerpt: And when the governor had read the letter, he asked of what province he was. And when he understood that he was of Cilicia; I will hear thee, said he, when thine accusers are also come. Already Lysias has spoken for his exculpation; but the Jews seek to gain the hearer beforehand. And he ordered him to be kept in custody in Herod's praetorium: again Paul is put in bonds.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 23:35
entries.excerpt: "In Herod's praetorium." It is noteworthy that in Caesarea there was a praetorium of Herod.
